goal: Drop structural.local_imports: aggregated style smell, ruff covers per-statement case, Python-only outlier in an otherwise multi-language ruleset. Doesn't drive non-trivial review action.

summary.title: Drop structural.local_imports
summary.what: Removed the structural.local_imports rule entirely — rule file, legacy kernel, test files, registry entry, default config, config template entry, language-specific test cases across c/cpp/ruby, skill SUMMARY row. Added to slop._compat.REMOVED_RULES so old configs error with a clear reason. 475 tests pass.
summary.why: Python-only outlier (other AI-supported languages don't exhibit the pattern); ruff PLC0415 already catches the per-statement case; the slop aggregate-count version didn't surface deeper structural debt — it measured count, not coupling. Same memory rule that drove the section_comments and information.* cleanups: rules that catch style smells agents readily fix should be cut entirely; only ship rules that drive non-trivial review action.
summary.user_goal: Drop structural.local_imports: aggregated style smell, ruff covers per-statement case, Python-only outlier in an otherwise multi-language ruleset. Doesn't drive non-trivial review action.

decisions.chose: Drop the rule entirely rather than port to v2.
decisions.rationale: Porting would invest in code that the standing 'structural debt, not style' rule says shouldn't ship. Better to spend the effort on rules that surface genuine structural debt.

decisions.chose: Add to REMOVED_RULES with reason rather than silent deletion.
decisions.rationale: Existing user configs referencing structural.local_imports will get a clear error message explaining why. Matches the pattern established for lexical.tersity, lexical.boilerplate_docstrings, information.section_comments.

decisions.chose: Don't add to LEGACY_CATEGORIES (no CLI alias preserved).
decisions.rationale: No legacy CLI invocation needs to translate; the rule never had a category alias to preserve. The REMOVED_RULES entry handles the rename-attempt case at config-load time.

review_notes: 475 tests pass — net -16 from removing the rule's tests across legacy and per-language test files. No v2 tests added (rule isn't being ported).
structural.* now has 16 rules across 4 sub-families and 8 standalones. local_imports was the only structural.* rule with a Python-only scope; removing it makes structural.* fully multi-language.
Three rules now in REMOVED_RULES: lexical.tersity, lexical.boilerplate_docstrings, lexical.identifier_singletons (v1.2.0 cuts) + information.section_comments + structural.local_imports (v2.0 cuts).

architectural_claims: structural.* is fully multi-language. Rules that target a single grammar's idiom belong in REMOVED_RULES (if they were style smells) or in a separately-scoped extension (none today).
behavioral_changes: structural.local_imports rule no longer exists. Configs referencing it will error with the REMOVED_RULES reason.
migration_notes: Users with [rules.structural.local_imports] in .slop.toml: remove the section. The rule no longer exists; use ruff PLC0415 for per-statement detection in Python.
